wikitext:
= Sicilian Defense, Dragon Variation, Yugoslav Attack =
{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|Yugoslav Attack||rd||bd|qd||rd|kd||pd|pd|||pd|pd|bd|pd|||nd|pd||nd|pd|||||||||||||nl|pl||||||nl||bl|pl|||pl|pl|pl|ql|||pl|pl|rl||||kl|bl||rl|parent=[[Chess/Sicilian Dragon|Sicilian Dragon]]}}Both sides have now sufficiently developed and white faces a major dilemma for their next move:

- 9. Bc4 was originally the most popular and critical line. White controls d5 as much as possible, trying to restrict all central counterplay from white. The mainline then follows 9...Bd7 10. 0-0-0 Rc8 11. Bb3 and both sides go on an all out attack on each other's king.

- 9. 0-0-0 was seen until recently a refuted line that allowed black to immediately equalize and avoid the massive pawn storm coming at them. With the advent of engines it was revealed that white actually maintains a larger, positional advantage. This led to a theory war with many top players trying to find deep ideas for each color up until the endgame.

- 9. g4 is a hyper aggressive variation that has the very simple and easy idea of punching black in the face as soon as possible by immediately going on the offensive. This variation is known for being extremely dangerous to both sides, but engine analysis has shown that black has sufficient counterplay in the center to survive the attack easily.
